Title: Set up staging server and install Docker
Description: Provision a VM or server for the staging environment (sso.mozhaiq.dev). Install Docker and Docker Compose. Confirm root/sudo access and open required ports (80 443 8080).

Title: Write docker-compose.yml for Keycloak and PostgreSQL
Description: Create the docker-compose.yml with Keycloak (latest stable) and PostgreSQL 16 services as defined in the plan. Include named volume for pgdata. Set restart: unless-stopped on both services.

Title: Configure .env file and secrets management
Description: Create .env file with KC_DB_PASSWORD. Add .env to .gitignore. Document secret rotation process. Never commit credentials to the repo.

Title: Boot Keycloak in production mode on staging
Description: Run docker compose up -d on staging. Verify Keycloak starts with start --optimized flag (not dev mode). Confirm Admin Console is accessible on port 8080 locally.

Title: Verify PostgreSQL connection from Keycloak
Description: Run docker compose exec postgres psql -U keycloak -c to confirm DB is up and Keycloak connected successfully on first boot.

Title: Configure nginx reverse proxy on staging
Description: Install nginx on the staging server. Write server block for sso.mozhaiq.dev with proxy_pass to localhost:8080. Set all required proxy headers (Host X-Real-IP X-Forwarded-For X-Forwarded-Proto).

Title: Obtain and install TLS certificate for staging domain
Description: Obtain TLS certificate for sso.mozhaiq.dev (Let's Encrypt or internal CA). Configure ssl_certificate and ssl_certificate_key in nginx. Verify HTTPS works end-to-end.

Title: Set KC_PROXY=edge and KC_HOSTNAME in Keycloak config
Description: Add KC_PROXY: edge and KC_HOSTNAME: sso.mozhaiq.dev to docker-compose.yml environment block. Restart container and verify Keycloak recognises the correct hostname.

Title: Verify Keycloak health endpoint on staging
Description: Confirm GET https://sso.mozhaiq.dev/health/live and /health/ready return 200. Set up an uptime alert on these endpoints.

Title: Repeat infrastructure setup for production
Description: Replicate all infrastructure steps (Docker Compose nginx TLS .env) on the production server for sso.kiebtg.app. Confirm health endpoints live.

Title: Create internal realm in Keycloak Admin Console
Description: Log into Admin Console. Create realm named internal. Confirm it is isolated from master realm. Do not use master realm for any apps.

Title: Create external realm in Keycloak Admin Console
Description: Create realm named external in Admin Console. Confirm both realms exist independently with separate login pages.

Title: Configure password policy for internal realm
Description: In internal realm: set minimum length 8 require uppercase lowercase digit hashing bcrypt and password history 5. Validate settings are saved correctly.

Title: Configure password policy for external realm
Description: In external realm: apply the same password policy as internal (min length 8 uppercase lowercase digit bcrypt history 5).

Title: Define realm-level roles for internal realm
Description: Create realm roles: employee and admin in the internal realm via Admin Console → Realm Roles.

Title: Define realm-level roles for external realm
Description: Create realm roles: customer and admin in the external realm via Admin Console → Realm Roles.

Title: Create app-provisioner service client in both realms
Description: Create a confidential client named app-provisioner in both internal and external realms. Enable Client Credentials grant and assign realm-admin role to its service account. Store client secret in .env.

Title: Create mobile-legacy-bridge client in external realm
Description: Create confidential client mobile-legacy-bridge in external realm. Enable Direct Access Grants (ROPC). Keep this disabled on all other clients.

Title: Document realm config and export realm JSON to git
Description: Export realm JSON from Admin Console for both internal and external realms. Commit to git repo as baseline config backup. Schedule weekly re-export.

Title: Verify bcrypt cost factor across all app codebases
Description: Inspect existing password hashes in each app DB: the number after $2b$ is the cost factor. Confirm it is consistent across all apps. Document the confirmed value.

Title: Add keycloak_id and sso_linked_at columns to all app DBs
Description: Run ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN keycloak_id VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE and ADD COLUMN sso_linked_at TIMESTAMP on each app database. Add index on keycloak_id.

Title: Write and test bulk import script on staging
Description: Implement bulk-import-users.js as defined in the plan. Set KC_REALM=external (or internal). Run against staging Keycloak (sso.mozhaiq.dev) with a test subset of users first.

Title: Verify test users can log in on staging via Keycloak
Description: Pick 3–5 test users from staging import. Log in via Keycloak login page using their existing password. Confirm authentication succeeds without password reset.

Title: Run full user import on staging (all users)
Description: Execute bulk-import-users.js against all users in each app DB targeting sso.mozhaiq.dev. Check import-errors.json and re-run for any failures. Confirm created count matches DB row count.

Title: Write and run backfill script on staging
Description: Implement backfill-keycloak-ids.js. Run against staging DB. Verify keycloak_id is populated for all users. Confirm zero NULL keycloak_id remaining for active users.

Title: Run full user import on production Keycloak
Description: Execute bulk import against production Keycloak (sso.kiebtg.app) for both internal and external realms. Review errors. Confirm all users exist in Keycloak.

Title: Run backfill script on production DB
Description: Execute backfill-keycloak-ids.js against all production app databases. Confirm keycloak_id written for every user. Spot-check 10+ rows manually.

Title: APM: Track import completion across all apps
Description: Create a simple tracking sheet: app name confirmed bcrypt cost import count errors resolved keycloak_id backfill complete. Update after each app is processed.

Title: Register Wave 1 app as Keycloak client
Description: In the appropriate realm create a new Client for the first app. Set Client ID protocol redirect URIs web origins and client authentication (OFF for SPA ON for confidential). Use exact redirect URIs.

Title: Integrate Wave 1 SPA with oidc-client-ts
Description: Install oidc-client-ts. Implement UserManager with correct authority client_id and redirect_uri. Wire signinRedirect and signinRedirectCallback. Store token in memory only — never localStorage.

Title: Implement JWT validation middleware on Wave 1 backend API
Description: Install jwks-rsa and jsonwebtoken. Implement verifyToken with JWKS caching (10 min TTL). Validate RS256 algorithm correct audience and correct issuer on every request.

Title: Implement SSO callback and keycloak_id lookup on Wave 1 app
Description: On the SSO callback route: extract sub and email from JWT. Look up user by keycloak_id. If found create session directly. If not found handle as new user creation.

Title: Define client-level roles for Wave 1 app in Keycloak
Description: In the Wave 1 app's Keycloak client define the required client roles following the naming convention app-{id}:{resource}:{action}. Assign roles to test users and verify they appear in JWT claims.

Title: End-to-end test Wave 1 SSO login flow
Description: Perform full login test: visit app → redirect to Keycloak → enter credentials → callback → session created → API call with JWT → authorized response. Confirm no regressions.

Title: APM: Communicate Wave 1 SSO rollout to internal users
Description: Draft and send internal notice about the SSO login change for Wave 1 app. Include what changes what stays the same and who to contact for issues.

Title: Register remaining internal apps as Keycloak clients (Wave 2)
Description: For each remaining internal app register a Keycloak client with exact redirect URIs client authentication settings and web origins. One client per app.

Title: Integrate Wave 2 SPAs with oidc-client-ts
Description: Apply the same oidc-client-ts integration pattern from Wave 1 to all remaining internal SPAs. Reuse the same pattern — do not invent new approaches per app.

Title: Implement JWT validation on Wave 2 backend APIs
Description: Apply the same JWKS-based JWT middleware from Wave 1 to all remaining internal backend APIs. Confirm correct issuer and audience per app.

Title: Define client-level roles for all Wave 2 apps
Description: For each Wave 2 app define client roles in Keycloak following the naming convention. Assign roles to appropriate users or groups.

Title: End-to-end test all Wave 2 apps
Description: Full login test for each Wave 2 app. Confirm JWT flow token validation and role-based access all work correctly per app.

Title: APM: Update internal team access matrix
Description: Update the permission matrix document (app vs group vs roles) to reflect all Wave 2 client roles now live in Keycloak. Share with team leads.

Title: Implement auth facade service (ROPC bridge)
Description: Build the auth facade as a Node.js endpoint: POST /api/auth/login. Validate credentials via Keycloak ROPC using mobile-legacy-bridge client. Return old token format wrapping Keycloak JWT.

Title: Implement version gate in auth facade
Description: Add App-Version check in the facade. Return 410 Gone with update_url for versions below MIN_SUPPORTED_VERSION. Wire the version check before the ROPC call.

Title: Add App-Version header to old mobile app requests
Description: Update old mobile app to send App-Version as a header or body field on every auth request. This enables the version gate to work correctly.

Title: Integrate new mobile app with AppAuth / PKCE
Description: Implement OIDC Authorization Code + PKCE flow using AppAuth-Android / AppAuth-iOS (or expo-auth-session / flutter_appauth). Wire to sso.kiebtg.app external realm.

Title: Test old mobile app through auth facade on staging
Description: Run old app version against staging facade. Confirm login succeeds. Confirm old token format is returned. Confirm app session works as before.

Title: Test new mobile app PKCE flow on staging
Description: Run new mobile app version against staging Keycloak. Confirm PKCE flow completes. Confirm native JWT received. Confirm API calls authorised.

Title: Deploy auth facade to production
Description: Deploy the auth facade service to production. Confirm routing from old mobile app is live. Confirm ROPC calls reach Keycloak production realm (sso.kiebtg.app).

Title: Register Wave 3 customer-facing apps as Keycloak clients
Description: For each customer-facing app register a Keycloak client in the external realm. Set exact redirect URIs web origins and client authentication. Double check settings before enabling SSO.

Title: Integrate Wave 3 SPAs with oidc-client-ts (external realm)
Description: Apply oidc-client-ts integration to customer-facing SPAs. Use external realm authority URL. Token stored in memory only.

Title: Implement JWT validation on Wave 3 backend APIs
Description: Apply JWKS middleware to Wave 3 backend APIs. Use external realm issuer and correct audience per app.

Title: Define client-level roles for Wave 3 apps
Description: Define client roles for all Wave 3 apps in the external realm. Assign customer realm role + app-specific client roles to test external users.

Title: End-to-end test Wave 3 apps with real external users
Description: Perform login tests with a sample of real external users. Confirm full flow from login to API access works. Confirm no disruption to existing sessions.

Title: APM: Communicate SSO rollout to external users (Wave 3)
Description: Draft and publish external user notice about the login change. Explain what changes what stays the same and support contact. Coordinate with product/support team.

Title: Disable local login form on Wave 1 app
Description: Remove or hide the local email/password login form on the Wave 1 app. SSO is now the only login path. Verify no local auth code path remains active.

Title: Remove password columns from Wave 1 app database
Description: Drop password_hash and related auth columns from the Wave 1 app's users table after confirming keycloak_id is populated for all users and SSO has been stable for 2+ weeks.

Title: Remove local auth dependencies from Wave 1 codebase
Description: Uninstall auth libraries (bcrypt passport etc) no longer needed. Remove middleware routes and config related to local auth. Clean up dead code.

Title: Repeat local auth decommission for all remaining apps
Description: Perform the same disable-remove-cleanup steps for all Wave 2 and Wave 3 apps. Work through apps one by one after each has been stable for at least 2 weeks.

Title: APM: Confirm decommission completion across all apps
Description: Verify all apps have local auth disabled password columns removed and unused deps cleaned. Update project tracker. Mark M8 complete.

Title: Monitor app store adoption rate for old mobile version
Description: Track percentage of active external users on the new mobile app version via app store analytics. Target: ≥90% before triggering hard cutoff.

Title: Add in-app deprecation banner to old mobile app
Description: At month 3–4 ship an update to the old mobile app showing an in-app banner: Please update your app by [date]. Link to app store listing.

Title: Enforce 410 Gone in auth facade for unsupported versions
Description: At month 6–9 (when adoption ≥90%) update MIN_SUPPORTED_VERSION in the facade to cut off old versions. Deploy. Confirm 410 response returned to old clients.

Title: Decommission auth facade service
Description: After cutoff is stable and no old clients are reaching the facade shut down the facade service. Remove the mobile-legacy-bridge client from Keycloak. Clean up related code.

Title: APM: Final mobile migration sign-off
Description: Confirm facade is decommissioned adoption is 100% on new app and no legacy auth paths remain. Update project documentation and close out the migration.

Title: Set up daily PostgreSQL backup via pg_dump
Description: Configure a cron job or scheduled task to run pg_dump keycloak daily. Store backup files off-server (S3 NAS or equivalent). Test restore procedure.

Title: Set up weekly realm JSON export
Description: Schedule a weekly reminder or script to export realm JSON from Keycloak Admin Console for both realms. Commit to git or store in secure backup location.

Title: Configure uptime monitoring and alerts
Description: Set up uptime alerts on /health/live and /health/ready for both staging and production. Configure notification to the team on failure (email Slack etc).

Title: Enable and configure Keycloak event logging
Description: Enable Admin Events and Login Events in both realms (Realm Settings → Events). Set retention period. Confirm login failure events are captured.

Title: Enable 2FA on all Keycloak admin accounts
Description: Enforce 2FA for all accounts with admin access to the Keycloak master realm and realm-admin accounts. Verify OTP setup for each team member with admin access.

Title: Create realm-admin user per realm for day-to-day ops
Description: Create a dedicated realm-admin user in both internal and external realms. Use these for daily operations. Reserve master realm admin credentials in password manager only for break-glass use.
